summary:
The second digit or index finger typically possesses two dorsal interosseous muscles based on common anatomic teachings; however, other digits like the middle may also fit this description depending on individual variation. Given ambiguity, selecting one definitive answer is challenging but aligns best with traditional medical education practices by opting for the commonly cited example—the middle finger (\(\boxed{\text{C}}\)). Summary:

The third digit (middle finger) usually contains two dorsal interossei muscles according to conventional anatomical instruction, although similar characteristics might apply to other fingers due to variability. To maintain consistency with educational standards, the middle finger (\( \boxed{\text{C}} \)) was selected despite potential ambiguities.
